title: KQL interpretation mini-task
prompt: A synthetic KQL-like query surfaces sign-in events where a successful login follows repeated failures from different documentation-reserved IP ranges.

artifacts.name: Synthetic KQL-like query
artifacts.content:
SigninEvents
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-05T09:00:00Z) .. datetime(2026-06-05T10:00:00Z))
| summarize failures=countif(Result == 'Failure'), successes=countif(Result == 'Success'), locations=make_set(Location) by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress
| where failures >= 2 or successes >= 1

artifacts.name: Synthetic query results
artifacts.content:
alex.chen@example.com ip=203.0.113.42 failures=2 successes=0 locations=[]
alex.chen@example.com ip=198.51.100.17 failures=0 successes=1 locations=['Unfamiliar']
jamie.lee@example.com ip=192.0.2.88 failures=1 successes=0 locations=[]

artifacts.name: Synthetic analyst note
artifacts.content: The query is for interpretation practice only. It does not query a real tenant and should not be copied into production without review.

questions.prompt: What does the query primarily summarize?

options.label: Sign-in counts
options.text: It summarizes failures, successes, and locations by synthetic user and IP address.

options.label: File hashes
options.text: It summarizes malware file hashes by endpoint.

options.label: Mailbox rules
options.text: It summarizes mailbox forwarding rules only.

questions.explanation: The query groups synthetic sign-in events by user and IP and counts failures and successes.

citations.title: Synthetic KQL Interpretation Lab
citations.excerpt: Synthetic KQL labs teach query interpretation with fictional logs and documentation-reserved network ranges.

questions.prompt: Which observations should be called out?

options.label: Multiple IPs
options.text: The same synthetic user appears with repeated failures and a later success from another IP.

options.label: Unfamiliar location
options.text: One successful sign-in has an unfamiliar location marker.

options.label: Identity context
options.text: The result needs identity/session context before final conclusions.

options.label: Confirmed breach
options.text: The query alone proves a confirmed breach.

questions.explanation: The correct interpretation calls out suspicious sequence indicators but avoids claiming proof from one query.

citations.title: Synthetic Incident Triage Playbook
citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.

questions.prompt: Explain the query result in plain language.
questions.explanation: Plain-language interpretation should mention repeated failures, the later success, and the need for more context.

citations.title: Synthetic KQL Interpretation Lab
citations.excerpt: Synthetic KQL labs teach query interpretation with fictional logs and documentation-reserved network ranges.

learner_task: Interpret the query, identify suspicious-but-not-conclusive signals, and explain the result in plain language.
expected_investigation_path: Identify that the query summarizes sign-in outcomes by user and IP.
Call out repeated failures followed by a success from a different documentation-reserved IP range.
Avoid overclaiming from a single query result.
Recommend identity/session context as the next defensive evidence source.

rubric.expected_signal: Explains the grouping and count fields correctly.
Identifies suspicious sequence and unfamiliar location without overclaiming.
States repeated failures, later success, and need for more context.

safety_note: Synthetic KQL interpretation only. The query is illustrative and not production guidance.

citations.title: Synthetic KQL Interpretation Lab
citations.excerpt: Synthetic KQL labs teach query interpretation with fictional logs and documentation-reserved network ranges.

citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.
